svg.text: Character abilities responding to user input via animations and node graphs
Skills encapsulate preset behaviors triggered by user input. Normal Attack, Elemental Skill, and Elemental Burst are all skills.
1. Calling character animations after skill casting
2. Triggering client node graphs via Node Graph Event Track at specific animation stages
Access via Skills tab in Combat Preset
Unique identifier, referenced when modifying skill config in Node Graph
on release based on branch tracks
Character will not fall off cliffs when casting displacement skills
Whether skill can be used while jumping, falling, or gliding
Forces skill cast even in states where skills normally cannot be used
Can Be Used When Hit in Mid-Air
Cast while being launched or in airborne hit animation
Number of uses; restores 1 per cooldown
Type and amount of skill resource
Radius and Height define search area
Radius, Height, plus filtering Angle and Rotation
Through Local Node Graph, search nodes retrieve target units.
Sector is an upgraded cylinder with angle/rotation filtering.
Destroy Skill When Reaching Usage Limit
Max uses during entire life cycle; auto-removed when reached
Timeline format: action track + logic track
Mark points, trigger Skill Node Graph
Animation defines the scale of the skill track. Total timeline length = sum of all animation durations.
Add events at corresponding animation timestamps to match visual effects with logic.
Follow Animation or Custom (cannot exceed anim)
Change which slot the animation is assigned to
Snap to configured time points on event/status tracks.
Toggle via settings button on right side of timeline.
Red lines: Animation start/end. Blue: preview points.
Repeats logic within a specified time range at defined intervals.
Or create loop track from New Track options
Configurable when Infinite Loop is off
Loop animation plays while skill button is held. If hold is maintained, animation continues up to duration limit.
If hold is released, animation interrupts and transits to branch based on branch track configuration.
Each branch = bundled animations. Skill transits to branch based on release timing.
Number of Branch Transitions divides track into segments. Each segment maps to a branch.
Adjust segment lengths to fine-tune transition conditions. Release timing determines which branch is entered.
Each branch can have multiple animation clips that play in sequence.
Multiple independent actions with combo response events. Two tabs: Combo Skill Animation and Charge Branch Animation.
Accepts pre-input; hold can transition to charge branch
Tap triggers response, transits to corresponding animation
Number of Response Phases, Response Transition Animation per phase
Pre-input Can Transition to Charge Branch, Successful Charge Duration
Plays first when entering combo from idle (tap or hold)
Charging Branch has extra slot (#2) between Global Pre-movement (#1) and Loop Animation (#3), only played from main branch transition.
3 animation slots filled as complete set (not individual).
Charging holds at last frame until player releases.
Air Skill slot available under Combat Preset > Player > Class > Configure Skills.
When character meets airborne conditions (height above ground), skill is forcibly replaced with Air Skill.
Once cast, animation enters Infinite Loop slot until Landing Branch conditions are met.
While within "Enter Landing Branch" state block duration, landing triggers transition.
Event blocks can be set to "Follow Infinite Loop Slot" to repeat with the animation.
Five skill slots determine UI display and input activation:
When a skill is added to a unit, a Skill Instance is created with a unique integer ID.
A single unit can have multiple instances with the same config ID. Instance must be linked to a slot for UI input.
Unlinked instances can only be triggered through Execution Nodes.
A single slot can contain multiple instances in a stack. Most recently added skill displayed with priority.
Air Skills and Ground Skills use independent storage structures. Active skill determined by ground/air state.
Node Graphs can bind/unbind instances to/from slots. Unbound instances retain uses and cooldown tracking.
XIV. Adding Skills via Node Graphs
Cast Skills via Server Node Graphs
Two main ways to add skills:
2. Via Node Graph (server or client nodes)
Tip: Adaptive Slots supported for Hold Skills, Normal Skills, and Air Skills.
Each slot supports both looping and non-looping animations. Click + to add slots; right-click to remove.
Warning: There is a very small probability that events will be skipped in the Node Graph Event Track.
Write key logic on the End Event Track as a fallback to ensure critical actions always execute.
